title: Law enforcement informal and formal settlement data and reports (Arkansas Municipal League)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Iowa Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Materials related to and sufficient to show all instances of alleged and actual inappropriate or illegal behavior or actions resulting in compensation or expenditures involving this agency.

Responsive materials include records of all settlements (formal and informal) and payments to which this agency was or is a party for the period from January 1, 1999 through the date this request is processed. Responsive materials include those for matters and legal claims resolved prior to the initiation of any litigation.

In particular, materials responsive to this request include all reports, lists, databases, spreadsheets, and other documentation detailing incidents of legal claims or other allegations requiring compensation for claimants, attorneys, or any other party.

If spreadsheets or similar documents are available in response to this request, please provide all segregable data, including but not limited to:

The nature of the claim or legal matter
The claimant and other parties involved
The relevant case and tracking numbers for court and internal systems
The date the lawsuit was served or litigation commenced
The final disposition or current status of the case, including whether a case is still pending
The amount spent to date as a result of the legal engagement
The amount paid to any parties related to the legal claim, including any city payout and any non-monetary compensation

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within business days the as the statute requires.

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Arkansas Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

VIA E-MAIL & U.S. MAIL  
FOIA Officer  
Office of the Governor  
State Capitol, Suite 250  
500 Woodlane Street  
Little Rock, AR 72201  

Subject: Arkansas Freedom of Information Act Request and Request for Executive Assistance

Dear FOIA Officer:

Pursuant to the Arkansas Freedom of Information Act (AFOIA), Ark. Code Ann. § 25-19-101 et seq., I submit the following request for records in the custody or control of the Office of the Governor.  In addition, because several public agencies have failed to comply with my earlier AFOIA requests, I respectfully ask the Governor’s Office to provide guidance or assistance—short of litigation—to secure timely compliance, as detailed below.

I.  Records Requested (January 1, 2023 to Present)

Please produce:

1. All correspondence (e-mails, letters, memoranda, text/instant messages, or other communications) sent to or from the Governor, the Governor’s staff, or any employee of the Office of the Governor that references or discusses:  
   a. Any public-records (AFOIA) request submitted by me, [Your Name]; or  
   b. The failure, delay, or refusal of any state or local agency to comply with an AFOIA request.

2. All internal notes, agendas, talking points, call sheets, or briefing documents prepared for the Governor or her staff concerning:  
   a. Compliance by Arkansas public agencies with AFOIA; or  
   b. Possible reforms, training, or executive action to improve AFOIA compliance.

3. Any policies, guidance, memoranda, or directives—draft or final—issued by the Office of the Governor from January 1, 2023 to the present that address:  
   • AFOIA response deadlines;  
   • Agency obligations to acknowledge and respond to requesters; or  
   • Non-litigation dispute-resolution mechanisms (mediation, ombudsman services, executive review, etc.) for AFOIA matters.

II.  Request for Executive Assistance (No Litigation Preferred)

On multiple occasions during the past several weeks I delivered written AFOIA requests (copies enclosed) to various public agencies seeking records of governmental actions that directly affect my family and the public interest.  Despite the statutory three-business-day requirement to respond “so far as practicable,” several agencies have provided no records, no timetable for production, and no specific exemption justifying nondisclosure.

As a private citizen with limited resources, I wish to avoid litigation.  Accordingly, I respectfully ask the Governor’s Office to:

1. Remind all executive-branch agencies of their obligation to timely comply with AFOIA, including the duty to:  
   • acknowledge receipt of requests,  
   • make records available within the statutory time frame, or  
   • provide written citations of specific exemptions for any withheld material.

2. Inform me of any existing state-level ombudsman, mediation, or administrative avenue that can assist requesters in securing compliance without court action.

3. Consider issuing or updating an executive memorandum or guidance that reiterates best practices for AFOIA compliance and encourages transparent, efficient handling of public-records requests.

III.  Form of Production

• Please provide all responsive documents in searchable PDF or their native electronic format; audio in MP3/WAV; video in MP4/MOV.  
• Deliver via secure download link or e-mail.  If file size is prohibitive, please contact me to arrange alternative delivery.

IV.  Fees

Because disclosure will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of government operations and is not sought for commercial gain, I request a waiver of any search, review, or duplication fees under Ark. Code Ann. § 25-19-105(d)(3).  If anticipated costs exceed $10, please notify me before processing.

V.  Partial Disclosure & Exemptions

If portions of the requested records are exempt, please redact only the exempt material and release the remainder, identifying each exemption relied upon as required by law.

VI.  Statutory Deadline

Please acknowledge receipt of this request.  Arkansas FOIA requires that records be made available within three (3) business days unless an exemption applies.  I appreciate your prompt attention.

Thank you for your consideration and any assistance you can provide.  Feel free to contact me at [phone] or [email] with questions or to discuss narrowing or clarifying the request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Christopher Reed

title: ST Francis Arkansas Courts (St. Francis County District Court)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Arkansas Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

VIA E-MAIL & U.S. MAIL  
TO:  • St. Francis County Circuit & District Courts – FOIA Officer  
     • St. Francis County Small Claims Court – FOIA Officer  
     • St. Francis County Family Court – FOIA Officer  
     • St. Francis County Clerk of Court – FOIA Officer  
     • St. Francis County Sheriff’s Office / Detention Center – Records Custodian  
     • Forrest City Police Department – Records Custodian  

Re: Arkansas Freedom of Information Act Request (Ark. Code Ann. § 25-19-101 et seq.)  
      – Records from 1 January 1980 to Present Concerning  
      Christopher Joseph Reed, Joseph Taylor, Kerri Reed, and Kendall Reed

Dear FOIA / Records Officer:

Pursuant to the Arkansas Freedom of Information Act (AFOIA), I hereby request access to and copies of the records described below. This is one identical request transmitted simultaneously to each agency listed above; each is asked to conduct an independent search of records in its possession, custody, or control.

TIME FRAME  
January 1, 1980, through the date this request is fulfilled.

REQUESTED RECORDS

A. Correspondence  
1. All correspondence—regardless of medium or format (letters, e-mails, text/instant messages, chat logs, memoranda, handwritten notes, or other communications)—exchanged between or among:  
   • Any judge, clerk, or employee of the St. Francis County District, Circuit, Small Claims, or Family Courts;  
   • Any employee, agent, or representative of the St. Francis County Prosecuting Attorney’s Office;  
   • Any employee, agent, or representative of the St. Francis County Public Defender’s Office or appointed defense counsel;  
   • Any officer or employee of the St. Francis County Sheriff’s Office/Detention Center or the Forrest City Police Department; and  
   • Any of the following persons or anyone acting on their behalf:  
       – Christopher Joseph Reed  
       – Joseph Taylor  
       – Kerri Reed  
       – Kendall Reed

B. Court Case Materials  
2. Complete court files for every St. Francis County District, Circuit, Small Claims, Family, or appellate case in which any of the individuals named above has been a party, defendant, witness, or subject.  This includes, but is not limited to: dockets, pleadings, motions, briefs, orders, judgments, sentencing documents, warrants, minute entries, exhibits, transcripts, and presentence reports.

3. All audio or video recordings of any court proceeding (in-person, remote, or hybrid) involving the above-named individuals, including arraignments, hearings, trials, sentencings, and post-trial matters.

C. Law-Enforcement & Jail Records  
4. All incident/offense reports, arrest affidavits, citations, investigative files, field notes, supplemental reports, evidence logs, booking sheets, jail logs, disciplinary records, and any other documents referencing or involving the above-named individuals.

5. All body-worn-camera (BWC), dash-camera, interview-room, or jail-surveillance videos and any related audio depicting the above-named individuals.

6. Any records (correspondence, plans, meeting minutes, briefing documents, logs, etc.) reflecting discussions or plans by law-enforcement or correctional personnel to arrest, detain, or surveil any of the above-named individuals before, during, or after court appearances.

D. Requests & Policies  
7. All requests for accommodations (ADA, language access, medical, scheduling, or other) submitted by or on behalf of the above-named individuals, and all responses, approvals, denials, or internal deliberations.

8. All discovery requests, FOIA/open-records requests, or other information inquiries submitted by or on behalf of the above-named individuals to any of the recipient agencies, together with responses, denials, or notes of non-response.

9. All written policies, manuals, directives, or memoranda (1980–present) governing:  
   • Communication among courts, prosecutors, defense counsel, and law enforcement concerning criminal defendants;  
   • Handling of accommodation requests;  
   • Planning or executing arrests in or around court facilities; and  
   • Preservation and disclosure of digital evidence (BWC, dash-cam, jail surveillance).

FORMAT & DELIVERY

• Provide responsive documents in searchable electronic format (PDF, Word, or native); audio in MP3/WAV; video in MP4/MOV or original native format.  
• Delivery preferred via secure download link or e-mail; if files are too large, please advise so we may arrange for encrypted media or in-person pickup.

FEE WAIVER / COST LIMIT

Disclosure is in the public interest and not sought for commercial purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees pursuant to Ark. Code Ann. § 25-19-105(d)(3). If costs will exceed $[amount you authorize], please notify me before incurring additional charges.

PARTIAL DISCLOSURE & EXEMPTIONS

Should any portion of a record be exempt, please redact only the exempt material and release the remainder. For each withholding, identify the specific statutory exemption and provide a brief explanation.

PRESERVATION & RESPONSE DEADLINE

Please preserve all potentially responsive records. Arkansas FOIA requires that you respond within three (3) business days of receipt of this request. Kindly acknowledge receipt and indicate the anticipated date of completion.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.  If clarification is needed, please contact me 

cc:  
• St. Francis County Prosecuting Attorney’s Office (for awareness)  
• St. Francis County Public Defender’s Office (for awareness)
```

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Christopher Reed

• Delivery preferred via secure download link or e-mail; if files are too large, please advise so we may arrange for encrypted media or in-person pickup.

FEE WAIVER / COST LIMIT

Disclosure is in the public interest and not sought for commercial purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees pursuant to Ark. Code Ann. § 25-19-105(d)(3). If costs will exceed $[amount you authorize], please notify me before incurring additional charges.

PARTIAL DISCLOSURE & EXEMPTIONS

Should any portion of a record be exempt, please redact only the exempt material and release the remainder. For each withholding, identify the specific statutory exemption and provide a brief explanation.

PRESERVATION & RESPONSE DEADLINE

Please preserve all potentially responsive records. Arkansas FOIA requires that you respond within three (3) business days of receipt of this request. Kindly acknowledge receipt and indicate the anticipated date of completion.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.  If clarification is needed, please contact me 

cc:  
• St. Francis County Prosecuting Attorney’s Office (for awareness)  
• St. Francis County Public Defender’s Office (for awareness)
```

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Christopher Reed

```
PARTIAL DISCLOSURE & EXEMPTIONS

Should any portion of a record be exempt, please redact only the exempt material and release the remainder. For each withholding, identify the specific statutory exemption and provide a brief explanation.

PRESERVATION & RESPONSE DEADLINE

Please preserve all potentially responsive records. Arkansas FOIA requires that you respond within three (3) business days of receipt of this request. Kindly acknowledge receipt and indicate the anticipated date of completion.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.  If clarification is needed, please contact me 

cc:  
• St. Francis County Prosecuting Attorney’s Office (for awareness)  
• St. Francis County Public Defender’s Office (for awareness)
```

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Christopher Reed

title: Cross County Courts etc. (Cross County District Court)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Arkansas Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

VIA E-MAIL & U.S. MAIL  
Custodian of Records  


Re: Public-Records / FOIA Request – 1980-Present Records Concerning  
Christopher Joseph Reed, Joseph Taylor, Kerri Reed, and Kendall Reed

Dear Custodian of Records,

Pursuant to the [Arkansas Freedom of Information Act, Ark. Code Ann. § 25-19-101 et seq.] (and, where applicable, the federal Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552), I request copies of the following records in your possession, custody, or control.

TIME FRAME

This request covers the period January 1, 1980, through the date this request is fulfilled.

REQUESTED RECORDS

1. All correspondence (letters, memoranda, e-mails, text messages, chat logs, instant messages, or other communications, including attachments) between or among:

   • any employee or agent of the Cross County Prosecuting Attorney’s Office;  
   • any sitting or former judge of the District, Circuit, Small Claims, or Family Courts of Cross County;  
   • any employee or agent of the Cross County Public Defender’s Office (or court-appointed defense counsel); and  
   • any city police, county sheriff, state police, or other law-enforcement personnel,

   that references, discusses, or otherwise relates to:  
   a. Christopher Joseph Reed;  
   b. Joseph Taylor;  
   c. Kerri Reed; or  
   d. Kendall Reed.

2. Complete court files—dockets, pleadings, motions, orders, judgments, minute entries, transcripts, exhibits, presentence reports, warrants, and any other filings or materials—from any District, Circuit, Small Claims, Family, or appellate case in which any of the above-named individuals is/was a party, defendant, witness, or subject.

3. All audio or video recordings of court proceedings (in-person, remote, or hybrid) from 1980 to the present involving any of the above-named individuals, including arraignments, hearings, trials, sentencings, and post-trial proceedings.

4. All law-enforcement incident reports, investigative files, case notes, arrest/booking sheets, jail logs, body-worn-camera or dash-camera videos, surveillance footage, and any other records referencing or reflecting plans, discussions, or actions to arrest or detain any of the above-named individuals before, during, or after court appearances.

5. All requests for accommodations (e.g., ADA, language access, medical, scheduling) submitted by or on behalf of the above-named individuals, together with any responses, approvals, denials, or related internal deliberations.

6. All discovery requests, record requests, or other inquiries submitted by or on behalf of the above-named individuals to the courts, prosecuting attorneys, or defense counsel, along with all responsive materials or documentation of any refusal, delay, or partial compliance.

7. Any internal policies, guidelines, directives, or memoranda—current or historical (1980–present)—governing:  
   • communication among prosecutors, judges, defense counsel, and law-enforcement concerning criminal defendants;  
   • the handling of accommodation requests; or  
   • procedures for planning or executing arrests in or around court facilities.

FORMAT & DELIVERY

• Produce records in native or text-searchable electronic formats when available (PDF for documents, MP3/WAV for audio, MP4/MOV for video).  
• Provide via secure download link or e-mail; if files are large, please advise so alternate delivery (e.g., encrypted USB) can be arranged.

FEE WAIVER / COST LIMIT

Disclosure will contribute significantly to public understanding of governmental operations and is not primarily in my commercial interest; I therefore request a waiver of search, review, and duplication fees under [cite state statute and/or 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(4)(A)(iii)]. If anticipated costs exceed $[amount you authorize], please notify me before incurring additional charges.

PARTIAL DISCLOSURE / REDACTIONS

If you determine that portions of requested records are exempt, please redact only those portions and provide the rest, citing each specific exemption and the legal and factual basis for each withholding.

STATUTORY DEADLINE & PRESERVATION

The [Arkansas FOIA] requires a response within three (3) business days (or the period specified by your jurisdiction). Please acknowledge receipt and preserve all potentially responsive records pending final resolution of this request.

Thank you for your prompt attention. If you need clarification, please contact me at the phone number or e-mail above.


Send a copy of this letter (adjusting the addressee block) to each of the following likely custodians:

• Cross County Prosecuting Attorney’s Office (city and county divisions)  
• Clerk of the District Court  
• Clerk of the Circuit Court  
• Clerk of the Small Claims Court  
• Clerk of the Family Court  
• Cross County Public Defender’s Office  
• Cross County Sheriff’s Office / any relevant municipal police departments

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Christopher Reed
